hypothesis.title: Enhance mitochondrial biogenesis to counter ELAC2-deficient prostate cancer cells
hypothesis.mechanism: Activating PGC-1α signaling restores mitochondrial function impaired by ELAC2 mutations, reducing oxidative stress–driven proliferation.
hypothesis.rationale: Hereditary prostate cancer linked to ELAC2 germline variants shows mitochondrial abnormalities; restoring mitochondrial health is an unexplored therapeutic angle.
hypothesis.next_test: Test PGC-1α agonists (e.g., ZLN025) on ELAC2-mutant prostate cancer cell lines for viability and mitochondrial respiration.

hypothesis.title: Dietary lycopene supplementation reduces circulating IGF‑1 levels and slows prostate tumor growth.
hypothesis.mechanism: Lycopene inhibits hepatic IGF‑1 synthesis via PPAR‑α activation, lowering systemic IGF‑1 that fuels prostate cancer proliferation.
hypothesis.rationale: Elevated IGF‑1 is linked to aggressive disease; lycopene has been shown to reduce IGF‑1 in animal models, providing a nutritional avenue to modulate this endocrine axis.
hypothesis.next_test: Randomized controlled feeding study of a tomato‑lycopene extract vs placebo in men with early‑stage prostate cancer, measuring serum IGF‑1 and Ki‑67 index.

hypothesis.title: Intermittent fasting mimicking diet modulates androgen receptor transcription in prostate cancer.
hypothesis.mechanism: Fasting-induced ketone bodies reduce androgen receptor (AR) expression and activity, potentially enhancing response to anti‑androgen therapy.
hypothesis.rationale: Obesity and metabolic syndrome are risk factors for aggressive prostate cancer; metabolic shifts can influence AR signaling, but diet‑based modulation of AR has received limited attention.
hypothesis.next_test: Phase II trial of a 5‑day fasting mimicking diet combined with enzalutamide in men with metastatic castration‑sensitive prostate cancer, with pre‑ and post‑treatment AR target expression profiling.

hypothesis.title: Repurposing antiplatelet agent clopidogrel to inhibit prostate cancer stromal fibroblasts.
hypothesis.mechanism: P2Y12 receptor blockade reduces fibroblast secretion of IL‑6 and CXCL12, dampening paracrine growth signals that support tumor progression.
hypothesis.rationale: Tumor‑associated fibroblasts express P2Y12; epidemiological data suggest antiplatelet use may lower cancer progression, indicating a microenvironment‑targeted strategy.
hypothesis.next_test: Ex vivo exposure of patient‑derived prostate cancer fibroblasts to clopidogrel, assessing proliferation and cytokine output, followed by a small pilot trial in patients.
